prose:
Fukui reactivity (f+ on the most electrophilic atom)
Method provenance: GFN2-xTB (GFN2‐xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method, no Gaussian basis), gas phase (no implicit solvent), charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (21 atoms), used as-is; no further relaxation. Property computed with chemkit skill “fukui-reactivity” (backend: xtb v??) via single-point calculations on the neutral (N), cation (N-1, doublet) and anion (N+1, doublet) states to obtain Mulliken charges and atom-condensed Fukui indices. SCF cycles converged in all three states. Most electrophilic site: Fe atom (index 11, 1-based) with f⁺ = 0.22885456 (dimensionless). Most nucleophilic site is the same Fe atom with f⁻ = 0.25938191.

Live log: the calculation was executed with the command
  mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 ferrocene.xyz …
which streamed to a live log at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/<timestamp>_fukui.out (available for inspection; it was announced during the run).
